title: grammitis millefolia
intro: Grammitis millefolia, also known as the fern-leaved grass-lily or Microlepia tateretii, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Grammitis millefolia include reducing fever, alleviating pain and swelling, and promoting wound healing, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine in various forms, including dried leaves and roots.
